key_insight: A transfer-learned physics-informed neural network can estimate lithium-ion battery states accurately while retaining the electrochemical structure of the SPMe and reducing retraining effort across chemistries.
engineering_relevance: This provides a faster surrogate for battery state estimation and voltage prediction, which is useful for digital twins, BMS design, and rapid adaptation to new cells or operating conditions.
method: Pretrain a PINN on general SPMe electrochemical dynamics.
Transfer learned weights to a target battery and freeze selected layers.
Fine-tune remaining parameters to estimate key internal variables and voltage.
evidence_signal: Validation with PyBaMM showed accurate voltage prediction and indicated improved generalization and faster training compared with training SPMe-PINNs from scratch.
watch_item: Transfer learning may still depend on how well the source battery and operating regime match the target, so poor domain similarity could reduce accuracy or stability.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Neural Network with Transfer Learning for State Estimation in Lithium-Ion Batteries using the Single Particle Model with Electrolyte
